name: TET2 CHIP canakinumab vascular-inflammation trial is recruiting without posted results
summary: NCT06691217 directly follows the CANTOS/TET2 branch with a prospective canakinumab vascular-inflammation and TET2 variant-allele-fraction design, but it has no posted results.
statement: ClinicalTrials.gov lists NCT06691217 as a recruiting randomized phase 2 trial of canakinumab versus placebo in 120 estimated coronary artery disease participants with and without TET2 CHIP, measuring 48-week vascular inflammation and TET2 variant allele fraction, with no posted results as of June 6, 2026.
population_or_model: Adults and older adults with coronary artery disease, including TET2 CHIP and non-CHIP groups
time_horizon: 48-week imaging and clonal endpoints with final safety assessment through week 60
quantitative_note: Estimated enrollment is 120 participants randomized 1:1 to canakinumab or placebo; half the trial population is planned to have TET2 CHIP.
caveats: Recruiting status and trial design do not provide efficacy evidence.
The primary endpoint is vascular inflammation by coronary CT angiography, not disease events, aging function, or mortality.
The TET2 variant-allele-fraction endpoint is important for clone-dynamics interpretation but has no reported result.
Canakinumab blocks downstream IL-1beta biology and should not be described as direct somatic mutation control unless clone-dynamics results support that.
